etymology.description: semantic 雨(雨) (yǔ, "rain") + phonetic 申(申) (shēn, "extend") -> lightning as rain's powerful emission -> representative of electricity
etymology.interpretation: 電(電) (diàn) combines 雨(雨) (yǔ, "rain") as a semantic component indicating the natural association with weather, specifically storms, and 申(申) (shēn, "extend") as a phonetic component that gives sound. The powerful visual and auditory impact of lightning during storms led to its conceptual link to the invisible force of electricity.
etymology.simplification: 电(電) simplifies by eliminating 雨(雨) (yǔ, "rain") and maintaining 申(申) (shēn, "extend") as a sole component with a reduced stroke count.

examples.chinese: 这台风扇需要电才能运作(這台風扇需要電才能運作)。
examples.pinyin: Zhè tái fēngshàn xūyào diàn cái néng yùnzuò.
examples.english: This fan needs electricity to operate.

examples.chinese: 我们准备了一根备用电线(我們準備了一根備用電線)。
examples.pinyin: Wǒmen zhǔnbèi le yī gēn bèiyòng diànxiàn.
examples.english: We prepared a spare electrical wire.

examples.chinese: 停电时我们用蜡烛(停電時我們用蠟燭)。
examples.english: We use candles during a power outage.
